AI at Work — Daily Digest
Generated 2026-03-18 13:02 UTC
==================================================

1. Learning to Present: Inverse Specification Rewards for Agentic Slide Generation
   https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16839v1
   → 1. Learning to Present offers professionals a more coherent and audience-aware way to generate presentations using AI.

2. Conductor – Multi-agent AI workflows in YAML with parallelism and human gate
   https://github.com/microsoft/conductor
   → 2. Conductor streamlines multi-agent workflows in YAML, enhancing parallelism and human oversight for efficient AI process management.

3. How AI's post-training process suppresses the creativity and whimsicality seen in earlier models, like GPT-2, leading to poor writing from many top AI models (Jasmine Sun/The Atlantic)
   http://www.techmeme.com/260318/p15#a260318p15
   → 3. Understanding the suppression of creativity in AI models like GPT-2 can guide developers in creating more engaging and diverse content generation systems.

4. Chronos: Temporal-Aware Conversational Agents with Structured Event Retrieval for Long-Term Memory
   https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16862v1
   → 4. Chronos addresses a critical gap in existing memory systems by enabling long-term structured event retrieval, improving conversational agents' performance over time.

5. langchain-ai/open-swe
   https://github.com/langchain-ai/open-swe
   → 5. langchain-ai/open-swe introduces an asynchronous coding agent that can enhance productivity for developers working with AI tools.

6. Show HN: AI Skills for Affiliate Marketing – Works with Claude, ChatGPT
   https://github.com/Affitor/affiliate-skills
   → 6. Show HN highlights a tool designed to leverage Claude and ChatGPT for affiliate marketing purposes, streamlining the process for marketers.

7. Polycode – self-hosted GitHub bot that runs AI agent workflows from issue labels
   https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47423086
   → 7. Polycode provides a self-hosted solution for running AI workflows from issue labels in GitHub, automating processes for teams managing multiple projects.

8. Internalizing Agency from Reflective Experience
   https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16843v1
   → 8. Internalizing Agency helps professionals understand how large language models can be designed to better align with human intentions and environments.

9. Prompt Programming for Cultural Bias and Alignment of Large Language Models
   https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16827v1
   → 9. Prompt Programming is crucial for ensuring that LLMs are culturally aligned and do not inadvertently propagate biases into their outputs.

10. Probing Cultural Signals in Large Language Models through Author Profiling
   https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16749v1
   → 10. Probing Cultural Signals allows researchers to evaluate the cultural bias present in LLMs, aiding in creating more unbiased AI systems that can better serve diverse populations.
